LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Review UI shows a mandatory data usage attestation checkbox (data_usage_attestation_checkbox) with the specified semantics: unchecked by default; text explicitly stating documents/snapshots/annotations are free of reuse restrictions; applies per document or per review session. The checkbox must be present on screens that allow persisting snapshots, generating gold, or exporting training data.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'When the attestation checkbox is not checked, user actions that would persist confirmations with document snapshots, generate gold snapshots, or produce training datasets are blocked. The UI surfaces a clear error and the backend returns a deterministic rejection response (e.g., 403/validation error).'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Server-side enforcement: attempts to store confirmations with snapshots, generate gold snapshots, or produce training datasets without an explicit attestation are rejected; audit logs record the attempt, user, document id, and reason.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'The workflow must not allow any bypass: automated/export jobs and APIs must validate attestation and fail if missing. Include automated tests (unit/integration) that assert blocked behavior and positive flow when attestation is present.'

LLM suggestion: Implementation guidance: include acceptance tests that verify default unchecked state, per-document/session scoping, UI presence, backend rejection semantics, and recorded audit entries for blocked attempts.

LLM advisory: Acceptance criteria are missing for this story; there are no testable statements that verify required attestation UI or enforcement behavior.
LLM advisory: Missing in-scope requirement: the review UI MUST include the mandatory data_usage_attestation_checkbox (INVARIANT INV-aca0b8f371dd9cbe) and this is not described in the story or AC.

LLM advisory: Missing in-scope enforcement: workflow MUST NOT store confirmations with document snapshots if attestation is not granted (INVARIANT INV-efc6a0ec97f0ee52) — no AC or implementation detail ensures this.
LLM advisory: Missing in-scope enforcement: workflow MUST NOT generate gold snapshots when attestation is not granted (INVARIANT INV-efc6a0ec97f0ee52) — no AC or implementation detail ensures this.
LLM advisory: Missing in-scope enforcement: workflow MUST NOT produce training datasets when attestation is not granted (INVARIANT INV-efc6a0ec97f0ee52) — no AC or implementation detail ensures this.
LLM advisory: Missing in-scope enforcement: the system MUST NOT allow bypassing the data usage eligibility gate (INVARIANT INV-ef0f9f226562ce1d) — no AC or implementation detail ensures this.

LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that explicitly require the attestation checkbox in the Review UI, e.g.: "Given a document opened in the Review UI, the page shows a mandatory 'Data usage attestation' checkbox that is unchecked by default; the reviewer must check it to mark the document as eligible for training/export."

LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that verify gating behavior, e.g.: "Given a reviewer completes edits without checking attestation, then the system does not store confirmations with document snapshots, does not generate gold snapshots, and does not include the document in any training dataset export."
LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that assert the attestation scope, e.g.: "Attestation is applied per document (or per review session) as specified and recorded in the audit log when granted."

LLM suggestion: Add an AC to prevent bypass: "Attempts to programmatically or UI-wise bypass the attestation/eligibility gate are rejected and logged; exports and dataset generation endpoints return 403 for non-attested items."

LLM suggestion: If this story is meant only to deliver UI visuals and edit actions (and not enforcement), mark the attestation/enforcement invariants as out-of-scope in the story description and link a separate enforcement story that will implement gating and export controls.

LLM advisory: Acceptance criteria missing or empty for this story. Without testable ACs the implementation cannot be validated against the story scope.
LLM suggestion: Add clear, testable acceptance criteria focused on the review UX scope. Example ACs:
LLM suggestion: - "Given a reviewer opens the Primitive Review overlay, when the document loads, then the overlay displays the primitive fields and edit controls for that document."
LLM suggestion: - "Given a reviewer edits a primitive and saves, when save completes, then the corrected output is persisted and the UI shows the updated primitive in the review state list."
LLM suggestion: - "Given a review session, when a primitive is accepted/marked complete, then the review state transitions to 'Completed' and is visible in the UI state column."
LLM suggestion: - "Given an edit action fails, when the reviewer attempts to save, then an inline error is shown and the previous stable state is preserved."

LLM advisory: Acceptance criteria are missing or untestable for the story scope: the story has no acceptance criteria describing expected, testable behavior for the Review State Engine & Priority Scoring (e.g., state definitions, mapping rules, UI behavior).
LLM suggestion: Add explicit, testable acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then form. Example ACs to include:

LLM suggestion: AC 1: Given a document with model confidence X and heuristic flags Y, when the engine evaluates the item, then the UI shows one of {Green, Orange, Red} (no numeric confidence displayed) and the chosen state matches the documented mapping rules.

LLM suggestion: AC 2: Given multiple items with differing states, when the reviewer sorts by priority, then items are ordered by internal priority score derived from state and heuristics (highest priority first) and this ordering is reproducible for the same inputs.

LLM suggestion: AC 3: Given identical inputs, when the engine runs, then the derived state and priority score are stable/deterministic and logged for auditability (location/timestamp of decision recorded).

LLM advisory: No acceptance criteria present for attestation gating or prohibitions that are directly relevant to event-sourced review/delta store behavior.

LLM advisory: Story does not describe enforcement that gold snapshots, training datasets, or stored confirmations are blocked when attestation is not granted (violates FORBIDDEN_CAPABILITY invariants).
LLM advisory: Story does not describe prevention of bypassing the data-usage eligibility gate (violates FORBIDDEN_CAPABILITY invariant).

LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that explicitly enforce attestation gating for all snapshot/dataset generation and storage flows. Example AC: "Given a review delta with attestation = false, when attempting to generate a gold snapshot or export a training dataset, then the system must abort generation and return a 'attestation required' error."

LLM suggestion: Add AC to prevent storing confirmations with snapshots unless attestation is granted. Example AC: "Given attestation is not granted, the system must not persist user confirmations together with any document snapshot or derived gold snapshot."

LLM suggestion: Add AC that the event payload includes an immutable attestation_status field and that downstream consumers (snapshot generator / dataset exporter) must check this field and fail if attestation is not granted.

LLM suggestion: Add AC that explicitly forbids bypassing the data-usage eligibility gate. Example AC: "Any programmatic/export path must validate the data usage eligibility gate and must be rejected if eligibility is not satisfied; privileged/back-end-only bypasses are not permitted."

LLM advisory: No acceptance criteria or implementation details enforce in-scope attestation gating. The story does not specify how to prevent gold snapshot generation, training dataset production, storage of confirmations with snapshots, or bypassing the data-usage eligibility gate when attestation is not granted.

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Given a document or review session where attestation is NOT granted, when the system attempts to create a gold snapshot, then the creation MUST be blocked and an auditable rejection event MUST be recorded.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Given a document or review session where attestation is NOT granted, when the system attempts to produce a training dataset or export, then the production MUST be blocked and the request MUST be logged with a reason.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'System MUST NOT store user confirmations together with document snapshots if attestation is NOT granted; attempts to persist such combined artifacts MUST be prevented and audited.'
LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'All code paths that create or mutate snapshots or produce training exports MUST validate the data-usage eligibility gate; bypass attempts MUST be rejected and logged.'

LLM advisory: Missing acceptance criteria: the story provides no testable acceptance criteria for the artifact regeneration and delivery behavior being implemented.

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "Given a user edits a document, when they save the edit, then the system deterministically regenerates all derived artifacts (corrected output files) and delivers them to the user automatically without manual rework, within X seconds."

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "Regenerated artifacts are versioned and discoverable: the UI shows the new artifact version, and an immutable audit log entry is created recording the edit, the regeneration timestamp, and the actor."
LLM suggestion: Add AC: "Regeneration is idempotent and deterministic: repeated identical edits produce byte-for-byte identical derived artifacts and do not create duplicate versions."

LLM advisory: Story lacks testable requirements ensuring the pipeline will not produce training datasets, gold snapshots, or store confirmations with snapshots when attestation is not granted, nor that the eligibility gate cannot be bypassed.

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "Given a review without attestation, when snapshot generation is requested, then gold snapshots are not generated and any snapshot stored must not include user confirmations."

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "System tests that attempt to bypass the eligibility gate (e.g., API flags, scheduled jobs, or admin overrides) must fail: given any bypass attempt without explicit attestation, the operation is rejected and an audit log entry is created."

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "Integration test: end-to-end pipeline verifies that with attestation granted, training export proceeds; with attestation not granted, pipeline ends after user-facing delivery and produces no training artifacts."

LLM advisory: Acceptance criteria are missing or empty for this story, so required behavior (graph assembly review: topology/connectivity checks, reviewer edits, validation and save flow) is not testable.

LLM suggestion: Add clear, testable acceptance criteria. Example ACs:
- Given a document with extracted graph primitives, when a reviewer opens Graph Assembly Review, then they can create, connect, disconnect, and delete nodes/edges and save the revised graph.
- Given a reviewer saves edits, when the system validates topology, then it flags disconnected components and prevents final save until warnings are acknowledged or resolved (specify expected validation rules).
- Given a saved revision, when viewing revision history, then the topology/connectivity changes are recorded with reviewer ID and timestamp.
- Include an AC for export/ downstream effects: e.g., 'Saving a reviewed graph does not trigger training dataset export or gold snapshot generation as part of this story.'

LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that explicitly require a mandatory data usage attestation checkbox in the review UI (unchecked by default) and define its semantics (INV-aca0b8f371dd9cbe). Example AC: 'Given a document in the review UI, the reviewer sees a data usage attestation checkbox unchecked by default; the reviewer must check it to enable export actions.'

LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that block export and downstream artifact generation when attestation is not granted. Example AC: 'Given attestation is not checked, attempts to export AAS/consumer packages, generate gold snapshots, or produce training datasets fail with a user-visible message and do not produce persisted snapshots or datasets.' LLM suggestion: Add acceptance criteria that explicitly forbid bypassing the data usage eligibility gate and include automated tests that attempt to bypass the gate (covers INV-ef0f9f226562ce1d).

LLM suggestion: Add test cases / validation steps: UI tests for presence and default state of the attestation checkbox; end-to-end tests showing export succeeds only when attestation is checked; negative tests showing no gold snapshots/training datasets are produced when checkbox is unchecked.

LLM advisory: Missing acceptance criteria: the story lacks testable acceptance criteria to verify recording of audit/provenance metadata (revision IDs, input hashes, model/config versions, attestation metadata, event-sourced history).

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Given a document revision, when the system ingests or a reviewer saves changes, then record an immutable revision ID, input hash, model and config version identifiers, attestation metadata (including attestation checkbox state and reviewer id), timestamp, and append an event to the event-sourced change history.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Given recorded metadata, when queried by auditors, then the system returns a tamper-evident audit trail showing the sequence of events and all recorded fields for that document revision.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'Given exported training data workflows, then exports must reference the recorded attestation metadata and must be blocked if attestation is not present (to be implemented by a separate gating story).'

warnings: Story does not follow 'As a [persona], I want...' format
LLM advisory: Acceptance criteria are missing for this story; there are no testable criteria to verify enforcement of attestation-based gating for training/export pipelines.

LLM advisory: IN-SCOPE invariant violation risk: story does not specify that production of training datasets must be blocked when attestation is not granted (produce_training_datasets_without_attestation).
LLM advisory: IN-SCOPE invariant violation risk: story does not specify that gold snapshot generation must be blocked when attestation is not granted (generate_gold_snapshots_without_attestation).

LLM advisory: IN-SCOPE invariant violation risk: story does not specify that storing confirmations together with document snapshots is prohibited when attestation is not granted (store_confirmations_with_snapshots_without_attestation).
LLM advisory: IN-SCOPE invariant violation risk: story does not specify that the data-usage eligibility gate cannot be bypassed (bypass_data_usage_eligibility_gate).

LLM suggestion: Add explicit acceptance criteria that verify attestation gating. Example AC: "Given a review where the attestation is NOT granted, when an export/training run is attempted, then the system MUST block generation of training datasets and return an error/blocked status."

LLM suggestion: Add explicit acceptance criteria for gold snapshots. Example AC: "Given attestation is NOT granted, when the pipeline attempts to create gold snapshots, then the operation MUST be prevented and no gold snapshot artifacts are created."

LLM suggestion: Add explicit acceptance criteria for confirmation storage. Example AC: "Given attestation is NOT granted, user confirmations MUST NOT be stored together with document snapshots; attempts to store them must be rejected or stored separately without linking to snapshots."

LLM suggestion: Add explicit acceptance criteria preventing bypass. Example AC: "There MUST be no API or administrative pathway that can bypass the data usage eligibility gate; attempts to bypass are blocked and logged/audited."

LLM suggestion: If this story will implement or change the review UI, include AC to add the mandatory attestation checkbox (INV-aca0b8f371dd9cbe). If the UI is out of scope, explicitly state that attestation state will be consumed from an existing UI artifact.

failures.message: data_usage_attestation_checkbox invariant missing or not verified in acceptance criteria

warnings: LLM advisory: Missing in-scope requirement: REQUIRED_FIELD 'data_usage_attestation_checkbox' is not covered by the acceptance criteria (story does not verify the mandatory attestation checkbox presence, default state, or per-document/session semantics).

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "The review UI presents a mandatory 'Data usage attestation' checkbox for the document (unchecked by default). When the reviewer marks 'Decline', the attestation value must be set to 'Declined' and recorded in audit metadata (timestamp + attestation) but the checkbox state itself must not be stored together with any document snapshot."

LLM suggestion: Add AC: "Verify UI behavior: the attestation checkbox is displayed and defaults to unchecked, and attestation applies at the document (or review session) granularity implemented by this story."

LLM suggestion: Clarify AC to explicitly assert: 'No gold snapshots, exports, or training dataset generation occur when attestation is not granted; no user confirmations are stored alongside document snapshots.'

failures.message: INV-efc6a0ec97f0ee52: store_confirmations_with_snapshots_without_attestation not addressed in acceptance criteria

warnings: LLM advisory: Missing in-scope requirement: acceptance criteria do not explicitly prevent storing reviewer confirmations together with document snapshots after attestation is revoked (violates INV-efc6a0ec97f0ee52: store_confirmations_with_snapshots_without_attestation).

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'When an attestation is revoked, the system MUST NOT store reviewer confirmations together with document snapshots for that document; any attempt to persist confirmations alongside snapshots MUST be blocked.'

LLM suggestion: Add AC: 'All export pipelines (gold snapshot generation, training dataset production, and any persistence of document snapshots) MUST check current attestation status and reject operations for documents with attestation status = Revoked.'
LLM suggestion: Optionally specify where revocation audit metadata is stored (e.g., immutable audit log entry) and that it is used by enforcement checks.
